text:
**4. [Blocker — Phase A/D sequencing] The lint exemption can't be finalized before the Phase D transport is decided.** Phase D leaves "declared projection **or** devtools channel" open. If receipts ride the normal FlatBuffer projection pipeline, the receipt schema becomes part of the (dev-build) app surface and the Phase A lint/ratchet wording must account for that; if it's a side channel, the exemption is purely dependency-graph-shaped. Suggested change: move the transport decision into Phase A / #2809 as a decision-gate item. [...] **5. [Should-fix — Phase A] "Compiled out of release builds" needs a mechanical definition and CI enforcement.** `cfg(debug_assertions)` is the wrong tool (release-with-debug builds exist; per-platform build configs differ). Suggested wording: nmp-devtools is a separate crate behind an off-by-default cargo feature; CI asserts each platform's release artifact dependency graph (`cargo tree`) and/or symbol table contains no nmp-devtools; the ratchet rule is dependency-graph-based — an allowlist file of crates permitted to depend on `trellis-*` (existing private adapters + nmp-devtools), where adding a line is the ratchet violation. [...] **Other findings that changed the epic:** capsules need a **symbolication manifest** (traces are keyed by opaque NodeIds — unreadable to anyone who didn't see the session without an embedded label registry); Trellis trace versioning is exact-match-only, so the capsule needs its own **envelope version + compat policy** or every capsule attached to an old issue dies on the first format bump; the Phase D **transport decision moved into Phase A** because the lint-exemption wording depends on it; free-form reason strings became **structured reason codes** (type lints can't catch a Debug-formatted `ResourcePlan` leaking into a string); the receipt shape gained the fields its own examples required (owner-count *transition*, outcome status, parent scope, timestamps); my "agent answers why-is-my-feed-empty" exit criterion was rightly called untestable and is now a **seeded failure-scenario corpus** against a local relay fixture — deliberately including the "subscription fine, zero events came back" case, which receipts alone can't answer; and the **headless NMP host runner** is now itemized as its own (probably largest) Phase C work item. [...] - **PR #2859** — the ADR amendment itself, closing #2809, with the lint classifier scoped to `crates/nmp-devtools/src` and a regression test. **Phase A: done.**
- **PRs #2860/#2861** — `XrayReceipt` + bounded ordered `XrayReceiptStream` + the Trellis→receipt adapter. Most of Phase B.
